title: Why Year-Round Pest Control Is Essential for Northern California Homes
author: Michael Valdez
desc: Year-round pest control is essential for Northern California homes. Learn how preventative pest control protects your home from ants, rodents, and more—bef
excerpt: When it comes to protecting your home in Northern California, pest control isn’t a one-time fix—it’s an ongoing strategy.
body:
When it comes to protecting your home in Northern California, pest control isn’t a one-time fix—it’s an ongoing strategy.

From hot, dry summers to cool, damp winters, each season brings its own wave of pest activity. Homeowners who wait until they see pests are already behind the curve. The truth is, by the time pests are visible, the problem has often been building for weeks—or even months.

That’s why year-round general pest control is one of the smartest investments you can make for your home.

The Reality: Pests Don’t Take a Break

Many homeowners assume pests are only a summer issue—but in Northern California, pest activity happens all year long:

  • Spring: Ants, spiders, and wasps begin to emerge and search for food and nesting areas

  • Summer: Peak season for ants, roaches, and outdoor invaders

  • Fall: Rodents start looking for warm shelter inside homes

  • Winter: Hidden infestations grow quietly in attics, walls, and crawl spaces

Skipping treatments during “quiet” months often leads to major infestations later.

Prevention vs. Reaction: Why Timing Matters

Most pest problems don’t start overnight.

A few ants in your kitchen could mean a colony is already established nearby. A scratching sound in the attic could signal rodents nesting and multiplying.

Reactive pest control (waiting until there’s a problem) often leads to:

  • Higher treatment costs

  • Property damage

  • Health risks

  • Repeat infestations

Preventative pest control, on the other hand, stops problems before they start.

With a consistent plan in place, your home stays protected—without the stress of surprise infestations.

What Year-Round Pest Control Actually Does

A professional general pest control service isn’t just about spraying—it’s about building a protective barrier around your home.

At Hunters Services, year-round pest control typically includes:

  • Exterior perimeter treatments to stop pests before they enter

  • Interior inspections to catch early signs of activity

  • Entry point identification & sealing recommendations

  • Seasonal adjustments based on pest behavior

  • Ongoing monitoring to ensure long-term protection

This proactive approach keeps your home consistently protected—no matter the season.

The Hidden Costs of Ignoring Pest Control

Pests are more than just a nuisance—they can cause real damage:

  • Rodents chew through wiring, insulation, and even plumbing

  • Termites silently destroy structural wood

  • Cockroaches and rodents can spread bacteria and allergens

  • Ant infestations can quickly spread throughout your home

What starts as a small issue can turn into thousands of dollars in repairs if left unchecked.

Why Northern California Homes Are Especially Vulnerable

Living in areas like Chico, Oroville, and surrounding communities means dealing with:

  • Warm climates that extend pest breeding seasons

  • Agricultural surroundings that attract insects and rodents

  • Seasonal moisture changes that drive pests indoors

These factors make consistent pest control not just helpful—but necessary.

A Simple Story Most Homeowners Relate To

A homeowner in Northern California noticed a few ants in their kitchen one summer. They wiped them away and didn’t think much of it.

By fall, those ants had turned into a full infestation—spreading to multiple rooms. At the same time, rodents had made their way into the attic as temperatures dropped.

What could have been prevented with routine service turned into multiple treatments, repairs, and frustration.

The Bottom Line: Stay Ahead, Stay Protected

Year-round pest control isn’t about over-treating—it’s about staying ahead.

When you maintain a consistent pest control plan, you:

  • Avoid surprise infestations

  • Protect your home’s structure

  • Maintain a healthier living environment

  • Save money in the long run

Protect Your Home with Hunters Services

At Hunters Services, we help Northern California homeowners stay protected all year long with reliable, preventative pest control plans.

Whether you’re dealing with ants, spiders, rodents, or just want peace of mind—our team has you covered.

Call 530-342-8950 today to schedule your inspection and keep your home pest-free year-round.

title: Why Rodent Problems Spike in Summer (And How to Stop Them Before They Start)
author: Michael Valdez
desc: When most people think of rodents invading their home, they picture cold winter nights driving mice and rats indoors. But here’s what many homeowners don’t
excerpt: When most people think of rodents invading their home, they picture cold winter nights driving mice and rats indoors. But here’s what many homeowners don’t real
body:
When most people think of rodents invading their home, they picture cold winter nights driving mice and rats indoors. But here’s what many homeowners don’t realize:

Summer is one of the most active seasons for rodent infestations.

Longer days, abundant food sources, and increased breeding cycles make rodents more aggressive—and more likely to find their way into your attic, crawl space, or garage.

If you’ve ever heard scratching in the walls or noticed droppings appear “out of nowhere,” summer may be the reason why.

☀️ Why Rodents Love Summer

During the summer months, rodents are in full survival and expansion mode:

  • Rapid breeding cycles (a single pair can multiply quickly)

  • Easy access to food (pet food, gardens, trash bins)

  • Dry conditions that push them toward water sources—like your home

And once they find a way in, they don’t leave on their own.

🐭 A Real Scenario We See All the Time

Last summer, a homeowner in Butte County called us after noticing a faint scratching sound in their attic.

At first, it only happened at night—easy to ignore.

A week later, it turned into constant movement. Then came the smell.

When we inspected the property, we found:

  • A small gap near the roofline—barely the size of a golf ball

  • Insulation that had been torn apart for nesting

  • Multiple entry points starting to form

What started as one small opening quickly turned into a full-blown infestation.

The kicker?

It could have been prevented with a simple exclusion repair early on.

🔍 What Is Rodent Exclusion?

Rodent exclusion is the process of sealing off entry points to prevent rodents from ever getting inside your home.

This is not just trapping—it’s long-term prevention.

Think of it as closing every door rodents could possibly use.

🛠️ Top Rodent Exclusion Tips for Summer

Here’s how you can protect your home right now:

1. Inspect Your Roofline & Eaves

Rodents are excellent climbers. Look for gaps, cracks, or damaged vents near the roof.

2. Seal Small Openings

Mice can fit through holes as small as a dime. Use steel wool, caulk, or metal flashing.

3. Secure Vents & Screens

Install rodent-proof mesh over attic and crawl space vents.

4. Clean Up Food Sources

  • Keep trash bins sealed

  • Don’t leave pet food outside

  • Harvest garden produce regularly

5. Trim Trees & Bushes

Branches touching your home act like highways straight to your attic.

6. Check Your Garage & Doors

Gaps under doors are one of the most common entry points.

🚨 Why Waiting Costs You More

Rodents don’t just “live quietly.”

They:

  • Chew through wiring (fire hazard)

  • Damage insulation

  • Contaminate spaces with droppings and urine

  • Multiply—fast

By the time you notice the problem, it’s usually already grown.

🛡️ Prevention > Reaction

The best time to deal with rodents isn’t when you hear them.

It’s before they ever get inside.

That’s where professional exclusion makes the difference—identifying the hidden entry points most homeowners miss and sealing them the right way.

📞 Protect Your Home This Summer

At Hunters Services, we specialize in rodent exclusion designed for long-term protection—not temporary fixes.

✔ Full home inspections

✔ Entry point identification

✔ Professional sealing & repairs

✔ Ongoing prevention strategies

Don’t wait until a small problem becomes a costly one.

📞 Call Hunters Services today to schedule your rodent inspection and keep your home protected all summer long.

title: 5 Signs You Have a Termite Problem Before It's Too Late
author: Michael Valdez
desc: Termites can silently destroy your home's structure for months before you ever notice. From mud tubes along your foundation to doors that suddenly won't cl
excerpt: Termites are one of the most destructive pests a homeowner can face — and one of the sneakiest. Unlike ants marching across your kitchen counter or a mouse scra
body:
Termites are one of the most destructive pests a homeowner can face — and one of the sneakiest. Unlike ants marching across your kitchen counter or a mouse scratching in the walls, termites can eat through your home's structure for months or even years before you notice anything is wrong.

Here in Northern California, termite activity spikes every spring and summer. The warm, dry climate across the Sacramento Valley and into the Oroville, Chico, and Paradise foothills creates ideal conditions for both subterranean and drywood termites. And if your home was built or rebuilt with fresh lumber — especially in wildfire recovery zones — you may be at even greater risk.

The good news? If you know what to look for, you can catch a termite problem early and save yourself thousands of dollars in structural damage.

Here are five warning signs every Northern California homeowner should know.

1. Mud Tubes Along Your Foundation

Subterranean termites build pencil-thin mud tubes to travel between the soil and the wood they're feeding on. These tubes protect them from exposure and keep moisture in as they move.

Check along the exterior foundation of your home, inside your garage, and around any concrete or masonry that contacts the ground. If you see thin, dried-mud lines running vertically along these surfaces, you're likely looking at an active or recent termite highway.

Even if the tubes appear dry or abandoned, it's worth having a professional inspection done. Termites often build new routes nearby.

2. Swarmers or Discarded Wings Near Windows and Doors

Every spring and early summer, mature termite colonies send out winged reproductives — called swarmers — to start new colonies. In Northern California, this usually happens during warm evenings after rain or irrigation.

You might notice a sudden cloud of small, winged insects near outdoor lights, or find dozens of tiny translucent wings scattered on windowsills, near sliding doors, or along the foundation. The swarmers themselves look similar to flying ants, but there's a key difference: termite swarmers have straight antennae, a thick waist, and wings that are equal in length. Flying ants have elbowed antennae, a pinched waist, and uneven wings.

If you're finding discarded wings inside your home, that's an even stronger indicator. It means swarmers emerged from within or very near your structure.

3. Hollow-Sounding or Soft Wood

Termites eat wood from the inside out. That means a beam, baseboard, or door frame can look perfectly fine on the surface while being nearly hollow inside.

Tap suspect areas with a screwdriver handle or your knuckle. Solid wood produces a firm, dense sound. Wood that's been compromised by termites will sound noticeably hollow or papery. In some cases, a screwdriver will push right through the surface with minimal pressure — revealing a honeycomb of damage underneath.

Pay special attention to wood that contacts or is near the ground: deck posts, porch supports, garage door frames, and subfloor joists in crawl spaces.

4. Frass — Tiny Pellets That Look Like Sawdust

Drywood termites — common in Northern California — don't build mud tubes. Instead, they live entirely inside the wood they're consuming. As they eat, they push their waste out through small "kick holes" in the surface.

This waste, called frass, looks like fine, six-sided pellets — almost like coarse sand or sawdust with a slightly granular texture. You'll usually find small piles of frass on windowsills, along baseboards, or beneath wooden furniture.

If you're sweeping up what looks like sawdust in the same spot repeatedly and there's no obvious source, drywood termites are a strong possibility.

5. Doors and Windows That Suddenly Stick

If your doors or windows start jamming, sticking, or becoming difficult to open and close — and it's not due to seasonal humidity — termites could be the cause.

As termites consume the wood in and around frames, they create moisture and structural warping. This subtle shift can throw door and window frames out of square, making them bind in ways they didn't before.

This is one of the most commonly overlooked signs because homeowners tend to chalk it up to the house "settling." If the sticking is sudden or isolated to one area, it's worth investigating further.

What to Do If You Spot Any of These Signs

If even one of these signs looks familiar, don't wait. Termite damage compounds over time, and what starts as a minor colony can turn into a serious structural issue within a year.

At Hunters Services, we've been protecting Northern California homes from termites since 1992. Our approach is built on Integrated Pest Management (IPM) — meaning we identify the specific species, locate the source of the activity, and apply targeted, eco-friendly treatment methods that solve the problem without unnecessary chemical exposure to your family or the environment.

Whether you're in Oroville, Chico, Paradise, or the greater Sacramento area, we offer comprehensive termite inspections that can catch a problem early — or give you peace of mind that your home is clear.

Think you might have a termite problem? Call Hunters Services today or fill out our online form to schedule your inspection. The sooner you act, the more you save.

Hunters Services Pest Control Family-Owned | Eco-Friendly | Serving Northern California Since 1992

title: How to Keep Ants Out of Your Kitchen This Summer
author: Michael Valdez
desc: Every NorCal homeowner knows the feeling — ants marching across the kitchen counter on a warm summer morning. From Argentine ants in Sacramento to odorous
excerpt: Prevention Tips From Northern California’s Eco-Friendly Pest Experts Every Northern California homeowner knows the feeling. You walk into the kitchen on a warm
body:
Prevention Tips From Northern California’s Eco-Friendly Pest Experts Every Northern California homeowner knows the feeling. You walk into the kitchen on a warm June morning, flip on the light, and there they are — a trail of tiny ants marching across the countertop like they own the place. By the time summer hits full stride in the Sacramento Valley, ant activity is at its annual peak, and kitchens are ground zero.

The good news? You don’t have to share your home with them. With a few smart prevention strategies and the right approach when things escalate, you can keep ants out of your kitchen all summer long. Here’s how our team at Hunters Services approaches it after 30+ years of keeping NorCal homes pest-free.

Why Ants Invade NorCal Kitchens Every Summer

Understanding why ants show up is the first step toward keeping them out. Northern California’s summer climate creates ideal conditions for ant colony expansion. As temperatures climb into the 90s and 100s across the Sacramento Valley, Butte County, and surrounding areas, ants ramp up foraging activity — and your kitchen offers everything they’re looking for.

What Draws Them In

•      Moisture: Sinks, dishwashers, and pet water bowls provide reliable water sources, especially when the outdoor landscape dries out in NorCal’s summer heat.

•      Food residue: Even small crumbs, grease splatters, and sugary spills are enough to trigger a pheromone trail that leads hundreds of workers straight to your countertop.

•      Easy entry points: Foundation cracks, gaps around pipes under the sink, and unsealed window frames give ants direct access from outdoor colonies to indoor food sources.

Once a scout ant finds food and marks the trail, the rest of the colony follows fast. That single ant on your counter is never really alone — it’s the advance team.

Know Your Ants: Common Species in Northern California

Not all ants behave the same way, and knowing which species you’re dealing with helps determine the best response. Here are the three most common kitchen invaders in the Oroville, Chico, Paradise, and Sacramento areas:

Argentine Ants

The most common household ant in NorCal. Argentine ants form massive supercolonies, travel in wide trails, and are drawn to sweets and moisture. They’re small, light brown, and persistent — a single colony can number in the millions, making DIY control especially frustrating.

Odorous House Ants

Named for the rotten coconut smell they produce when crushed, odorous house ants are another frequent kitchen visitor in our region. They nest near moisture sources and prefer sugary foods. You’ll often find them trailing along countertop edges and around dishwashers.

Pavement Ants

Typically nesting outdoors under driveways, walkways, and foundations, pavement ants migrate indoors during peak summer heat. They’re less picky than Argentine ants and will eat greasy or protein-based foods in addition to sweets.

💡 Identification Tip

If you’re seeing large, dark ants (especially near wood), you may be dealing with carpenter ants — which can cause structural damage. Contact a pest professional for a proper assessment.

7 Ways to Keep Ants Out of Your Kitchen This Summer

Prevention is always the most effective approach — and the most eco-friendly. These are the same strategies our technicians recommend to homeowners across Northern California:

1. Eliminate Food Sources

Wipe countertops after every meal, sweep floors daily, and store pantry staples like sugar, flour, cereal, and pet food in sealed airtight containers. Pay special attention to sticky residue from honey, syrup, and fruit juice — these are ant magnets.

2. Cut Off Water Access

Fix dripping faucets, dry out sinks before bed, and don’t leave standing water in pet bowls overnight. In NorCal’s dry summer climate, your kitchen sink may be the most reliable water source for miles of ant colonies.

3. Seal Entry Points

Inspect your kitchen’s baseboards, window frames, and the areas where plumbing enters the wall. Use caulk to seal cracks and gaps. A tube of silicone caulk and 20 minutes of your time can eliminate the most common access routes.

4. Clean the Hidden Spots

Ants don’t just travel across countertops. Pull out the toaster, clean behind the microwave, wipe down cabinet interiors, and check under the fridge. Old crumbs and grease in these overlooked spots are often what sustains a trail.

5. Manage Outdoor Attractants

Move trash cans and compost bins away from exterior kitchen walls. Trim shrubs and tree branches that touch the house — ants use these as bridges. Keep mulch pulled back at least 12 inches from the foundation.

6. Use Natural Deterrents Strategically

Vinegar-water solutions (50/50 mix) sprayed along ant trails can disrupt pheromone paths temporarily. Peppermint oil near entry points may discourage scouts. These aren’t permanent solutions, but they can buy time while you address the root cause.

7. Don’t Ignore the First Scout

When you see that first lone ant wandering your countertop, act immediately. Clean the area thoroughly, check for entry points nearby, and remove whatever attracted it. Waiting until you see a full trail means the colony has already committed.

“Prevention is always the most effective and eco-friendly approach to ant control. A little maintenance goes a long way toward a pest-free kitchen all summer.”

When DIY Isn’t Enough: Signs You Need Professional Help

Sometimes prevention and home remedies aren’t enough — especially with large or established colonies. Here’s when it’s time to call in a professional pest management team:

•      Recurring trails: If ants keep coming back after repeated cleaning and sealing, the colony is likely nesting inside or directly adjacent to your home.

•      Multiple entry points: Ants appearing in several rooms simultaneously suggests a larger infestation that needs targeted treatment.

•      Carpenter ant signs: Sawdust-like frass near wood, large black ants, or hollow-sounding trim boards indicate a species that damages structures.

•      Outdoor super colonies: Argentine ant supercolonies in NorCal can span entire properties. Professional exterior treatments create a barrier that DIY products can’t match.

How Hunters Services Handles Ant Control Differently

At Hunters Services, we don’t just spray and hope for the best. As the first eco-certified pest control company in Northern California, our approach is built on Integrated Pest Management — a system that targets the root cause of your ant problem while minimizing environmental impact.

Here’s what that looks like in practice:

•      Thorough inspection: We identify the species, locate entry points, and trace trails back to the colony source.

•      Targeted treatment: Rather than broad chemical application, we use species-specific baits, insect growth regulators, and eco-friendly products that solve the problem at the colony level.

•      Exclusion recommendations: We show you exactly where ants are getting in and help seal those access points for long-term prevention.

•      Ongoing protection: Our customized treatment plans include follow-up visits to ensure the problem stays solved through peak summer months and beyond.

We’ve been doing this across Oroville, Chico, Paradise, Sacramento, Redding, and surrounding communities for over 30 years. We know the local ant species, we know NorCal’s seasonal rhythms, and we know how to protect your home without compromising the environment.

Ready to Enjoy a Pest-Free Summer?

Hunters Services offers free estimates for ant control and all pest management services across Northern California. Family owned, eco-friendly, and trusted by over 50,000 homeowners.

Call (530) 342-8950  |  Sacramento: (916) 273-8911

hunterspestcontrol.com

title: Keeping Your Home Up to Date: The Key to Protecting What Matters Most
author: Michael Valdez
desc: our home is more than just walls and a roof — it’s where life happens. It’s where you relax after long days, host family dinners, and build memories.
body:
Your home is more than just walls and a roof — it’s where life happens. It’s where you relax after long days, host family dinners, and build memories.

But just like anything valuable, your home needs consistent care to stay safe, efficient, and problem-free.

The good news? Most major home repairs can be prevented with simple, routine maintenance. A little attention throughout the year can save you thousands of dollars — and a whole lot of stress — down the road.

Let’s walk through the most important areas homeowners should stay on top of.

1. Gutter Cleaning: Protecting Your Roof & Foundation

Gutters play a bigger role than most people realize. When they’re working properly, they direct rainwater away from your roof, siding, and foundation.

When they’re clogged? That’s when problems start.

What can happen with clogged gutters:

  • Water backing up under shingles

  • Wood rot along fascia boards

  • Soil erosion around your foundation

  • Basement or crawl space moisture issues

  • Increased pest activity in standing debris

Homeowner Tip: Inspect your gutters at least twice a year — especially in the fall and spring. If you notice sagging, overflow during rain, or visible debris buildup, it’s time for a cleaning.

2. Termite Inspections: Catching Damage Early

Termites are silent destroyers. Most homeowners don’t realize they have a problem until significant structural damage has already occurred.

In Northern California, termite activity is common — and prevention is far more affordable than repairs.

Signs you might have termites:

  • Mud tubes along foundations

  • Hollow-sounding wood

  • Discarded wings near windows or doors

  • Bubbling or peeling paint

Homeowner Tip: Schedule a professional termite inspection annually. Even if everything looks fine, early detection can prevent thousands in structural damage.

3. Weed Abatement: More Than Just Curb Appeal

Overgrown weeds aren’t just unsightly — they can become a serious fire hazard, especially during dry seasons.

Weeds can also:

  • Attract rodents and insects

  • Damage to fencing and siding

  • Spread into landscaping and lawns

  • Violate local property maintenance requirements

Homeowner Tip: Regular trimming, clearing dry brush, and maintaining defensible space around your home can dramatically reduce fire risk and pest issues.

4. Routine Pest Control: Prevention Is Easier Than Infestation

Ants in the kitchen. Spiders in the garage. Rodents in the attic.

Many pest issues start small and grow quickly. Regular pest control treatments act as a protective barrier around your home — stopping problems before they become expensive infestations.

Common entry points homeowners overlook:

  • Gaps around utility lines

  • Cracks in the foundation vents

  • Worn weather stripping

  • Garage door seals

Homeowner Tip: Quarterly pest control service is one of the simplest ways to maintain peace of mind year-round.

5. Roof & Exterior Inspections: Your First Line of Defense

Your roof shields everything underneath it. Small issues — like cracked shingles or minor flashing damage — can turn into leaks that impact insulation, drywall, and electrical systems.

Walk around your home periodically and look for:

  • Missing shingles

  • Damaged siding

  • Cracks in stucco

  • Soft spots in wood trim

Catching exterior wear early keeps repairs small and manageable.

6. Foundation & Drainage Checks

Water is one of the biggest threats to a home’s structural integrity. Poor drainage around your property can lead to:

  • Foundation cracks

  • Mold growth

  • Pest attraction

  • Soil shifting

Make sure downspouts direct water away from your home and that soil slopes gently away from the foundation.

7. Seasonal Maintenance Checklist

A simple seasonal approach makes home care manageable.

Spring & Summer

  • Gutter cleaning

  • Pest barrier treatment

  • Weed control & brush clearing

  • Termite inspection

Fall & Winter

  • Roof inspection

  • Seal cracks & entry points

  • Check drainage systems

  • Rodent prevention

Breaking maintenance into seasons prevents overwhelm and spreads out costs.

Comfortable Homes Start With Consistent Care

Home maintenance doesn’t have to feel stressful or complicated. It’s really about creating a simple routine that keeps small issues from turning into major problems.

When you stay proactive:

  • Repairs cost less

  • Your home value stays protected

  • Your family stays comfortable

  • You avoid surprise emergencies

If you’re unsure where to start — or just don’t have the time — that’s what we’re here for.

At Hunter’s Services, we help homeowners stay ahead of issues with termite inspections, pest control, weed abatement, gutter cleaning, and more.

Let us help keep your home protected, safe, and running smoothly year-round.

📞 Call Hunter’s Services today to schedule your inspection or service.

Because peace of mind at home is worth it.

title: Fun in the sun, pest free!
author: Michael Valdez
desc: Summer is a time for fun in the sun, but unfortunately, it's also a time when pests can be more prevalent. With the warm weather and increased humidity, pe
excerpt: Summer is a time for fun in the sun, but unfortunately, it's also a time when pests can be more prevalent. With the warm weather and increased humidity, pests s
body:
Summer is a time for fun in the sun, but unfortunately, it's also a time when pests can be more prevalent. With the warm weather and increased humidity, pests such as mosquitoes, ants, and flies can become a real nuisance. Here are some tips on how to keep pests away during the summer months.

  • Seal Up Cracks and Holes

Pests can easily make their way into your home through cracks and holes in the walls or foundation. Seal up any openings you find with caulk or another sealant to prevent them from coming inside.

  • Keep Your Home Clean

Keeping your home clean is essential in preventing pests from taking up residence. Clean up spills and crumbs immediately, and don't leave dishes in the sink overnight. Sweep and mop regularly to keep your floors free of debris.

  • Properly Store Food

Pests are attracted to food, so it's important to store it properly. Keep food in airtight containers, and make sure to throw away any expired items. Don't leave pet food out overnight, either.

  • Maintain Your Yard

Your yard can be a breeding ground for pests, so it's important to maintain it properly. Keep your grass trimmed, and remove any standing water that can attract mosquitoes. Don't leave piles of leaves or other debris lying around, as they can provide a place for pests to hide.

  • Install Screens

Installing screens on your doors and windows is a great way to keep pests out while still allowing fresh air to circulate. Make sure to repair any tears or holes in the screens as soon as you notice them.

  • Use Natural Repellents

There are many natural repellents that can help keep pests away. Citronella candles and essential oils such as peppermint, eucalyptus, and lavender can all help repel mosquitoes and other pests. You can also try planting herbs such as basil and mint around your home, as they can act as natural repellents.

  • Call a Professional

If you're still struggling with pests, it may be time to call in a professional. Our experts at Hunters Services can help you identify the source of the problem and come up with a plan to eliminate the pests for good.

By following these tips, you can keep pests away and enjoy a pest-free summer. Don't let unwanted visitors ruin your fun in the sun!

title: Keeping Mosquitoes at Bay: Exploring Effective Strategies and the In2Care System
author: Michael Valdez
desc: With their itchy bites and potential to transmit diseases, mosquitoes are unwelcome visitors in any outdoor space. As the weather warms up and summer appro
excerpt: With their itchy bites and potential to transmit diseases, mosquitoes are unwelcome visitors in any outdoor space. As the weather warms up and summer approaches
body:
With their itchy bites and potential to transmit diseases, mosquitoes are unwelcome visitors in any outdoor space. As the weather warms up and summer approaches, it becomes even more important to find effective methods to keep these pesky insects away. In this blog post, we will delve into some practical tips for mosquito control and discuss the innovative In2Care system as a promising solution.

Understanding Mosquito Behavior:

Before diving into prevention methods, it's essential to understand mosquito behavior. Mosquitoes are attracted to humans and animals by the carbon dioxide we exhale, as well as our body heat and certain scents. They thrive in warm and humid environments, breeding in stagnant water sources like ponds, puddles, and even the smallest containers. By disrupting their breeding cycles and deterring them from biting, we can significantly reduce mosquito populations.

Eliminate Breeding Sites:

The first step in mosquito control is to remove or minimize their breeding sites around your property. Here are a few key actions to take:

  • Regularly empty and clean any containers that collect water, such as flower pots, birdbaths, and gutters.

  • Ensure that your rain gutters are free of debris to prevent water accumulation.

  • Repair any leaks or issues with outdoor faucets, pipes, or sprinklers that may create standing water.

  • Keep swimming pools clean and properly chlorinated.

  • Change the water in pet bowls, vases, and other water-holding vessels frequently.

Maintain a Mosquito-Repellent Landscape:

Certain plants naturally repel mosquitoes due to their fragrance or chemical compounds. Consider incorporating the following plants into your landscape:

  • Citronella: This popular plant is known for its strong scent that repels mosquitoes.

  • Lavender: Its pleasant aroma repels mosquitoes and other insects.

  • Marigold: The scent of marigolds acts as a natural deterrent to mosquitoes.

  • Catnip: Catnip contains nepetalactone, an essential oil that mosquitoes find repulsive.

  • Lemon balm: The lemony scent of this plant can help repel mosquitoes.

Use Physical Barriers:

Creating physical barriers can significantly reduce mosquito encounters. Here are some effective strategies:

  • Install window screens with fine mesh to prevent mosquitoes from entering your home.

  • Use mosquito netting around outdoor seating areas, particularly during peak mosquito activity times.

  • Wear long-sleeved shirts, long pants, and socks when spending time outdoors, especially during dawn and dusk when mosquitoes are most active.

Employ Mosquito-Repellent Products:

Various mosquito-repellent products are available in the market, such as sprays, lotions, and candles. Look for those containing DEET, picaridin, or oil of lemon eucalyptus (OLE), as they have been proven to be effective. Apply repellents according to the instructions provided by the manufacturer.

The In2Care System: An Innovative Solution:

The In2Care system is a revolutionary mosquito control solution that targets the Aedes mosquito species, known for transmitting diseases like dengue fever, Zika virus, and chikungunya. Here's how it works:

  1. Traps: The In2Care system employs specially designed mosquito traps that lure in mosquitoes using artificial human scents, body heat mimicry, and water as a breeding site.

  2. Contamination: The mosquitoes that enter the traps pick up a slow-acting biological larvicide and a powdered fungus. The larvicide kills the mosquito larvae and the fungus adheres to the mosquito, acting as a secondary killer.

  3. Transmission: The contaminated mosquitoes fly out of the trap to seek other breeding sites, transmitting the larvicide

Get in touch with us!

If you're tired of mosquitoes getting the best of you and your family, give Hunter's Services a call today at 530-342-8950 or contact us here for a quote today!

title: Definitive Guide: How to Prevent Bed Bugs and Ensure Peaceful Sleep
author: Michael Valdez
desc: Bed bugs, the tiny nocturnal pests that can wreak havoc on your peaceful slumber, have been a persistent nuisance for centuries.
excerpt: Bed bugs, the tiny nocturnal pests that can wreak havoc on your peaceful slumber, have been a persistent nuisance for centuries. These elusive creatures are not
body:
Bed bugs, the tiny nocturnal pests that can wreak havoc on your peaceful slumber, have been a persistent nuisance for centuries. These elusive creatures are notorious for their ability to infest homes, causing discomfort, anxiety, and financial strain. The good news is that with some proactive steps and vigilance, you can significantly reduce the risk of a bed bug infestation. In this comprehensive guide, we'll explore effective strategies to prevent bed bugs and ensure your bedroom remains a sanctuary for restful sleep.

  1. Understand Bed Bugs

Knowledge is your first line of defense. Educate yourself about the appearance, behavior, and habits of bed bugs. These flat, reddish-brown insects are about the size of an apple seed and are often found hiding in cracks, crevices, and seams of mattresses, box springs, and furniture. Knowing what to look for will help you detect their presence early.

  1. Inspect Second-Hand Items

Before bringing used furniture, clothing, or other items into your home, carefully inspect them for any signs of bed bugs. Look for rust-colored stains, tiny eggs, shed exoskeletons, or live bugs. Heat treatment, steam cleaning, or vacuuming can help eliminate potential hitchhikers.

  1. Encase Your Mattress and Box Spring

Invest in high-quality, bed bug-proof mattress and box spring encasements. These specially designed covers create a barrier that prevents bed bugs from infesting or escaping from these crucial sleeping areas. Regularly inspect and clean these encasements to ensure their effectiveness.

  1. Declutter and Maintain a Clean Environment

Minimize clutter in your living space to reduce potential hiding spots for bed bugs. Regularly vacuum carpets, rugs, upholstery, and curtains using a strong suction and a brush attachment. Pay close attention to cracks and crevices where bed bugs might hide. After vacuuming, seal and dispose of the vacuum bag properly.

  1. Launder with Care

Wash and dry your bedding, linens, and clothing at high temperatures, as heat kills bed bugs and their eggs. Keep your bedroom tidy, and avoid leaving clothes or other items on the floor.

  1. Practice Travel Precautions

When traveling, inspect hotel rooms thoroughly for signs of bed bugs. Place your luggage on luggage racks or in the bathroom rather than on the bed. After returning home, launder your travel clothing and vacuum your luggage before storing it.

  1. Be Cautious with Guests

If you have visitors staying over, take precautions to prevent potential bed bug introductions. Provide a designated area for their belongings away from your sleeping area. After their departure, inspect and clean the space they occupied.

  1. Regular Inspections

Perform routine inspections of your sleeping area, especially the mattress seams, headboard, and nearby furniture. Early detection is crucial for preventing a small infestation from becoming a full-blown problem.

  1. Professional Pest Control

If you suspect a bed bug infestation or have encountered them in your home, consult with us here at Hunter's Services. We have the expertise and tools to assess the situation, recommend treatment options, and ensure thorough eradication.

Preventing bed bugs requires consistent effort and vigilance, but the peace of mind and restful sleep it brings are well worth it. By adopting a proactive approach, practicing good hygiene, and staying informed, you can significantly reduce the risk of a bed bug infestation and enjoy your home as the haven it's meant to be. Remember, knowledge and prevention are the keys to maintaining a bed bug-free environment.

title: Summer Spider Control: Keeping Arachnids and Black Widows at Bay
author: Michael Valdez
desc: As the summer months arrive, so do the unwelcome guests - spiders and, for some regions, the notorious black widows.
excerpt: As the summer months arrive, so do the unwelcome guests - spiders and, for some regions, the notorious black widows. While these eight-legged creatures play a c
body:
As the summer months arrive, so do the unwelcome guests - spiders and, for some regions, the notorious black widows. While these eight-legged creatures play a crucial role in maintaining the ecosystem, encountering them in and around our homes can be unsettling and, in some cases, potentially dangerous. This blog aims to provide you with practical tips to keep spiders and black widows under control during the summer without harming them unnecessarily.

  1. Seal and Secure Your Home

The first line of defense against spiders and black widows is to prevent their entry into your home. Inspect the exterior of your house for cracks, gaps, and holes in windows, doors, and foundations. Use weather stripping and caulking to seal any openings that could serve as entry points for these arachnids. Ensuring your home is well-protected will reduce the chances of encountering them indoors.

  1. Keep Your Home Clean and Clutter-Free

Spiders are drawn to cluttered and messy environments as they offer plenty of hiding spots. Regularly declutter your living spaces and storage areas, eliminating unnecessary items that can become spider havens. Vacuum and sweep regularly to remove cobwebs and spider egg sacs. Pay particular attention to corners, ceilings, and dark areas.

  1. Reduce Outdoor Lighting

Excessive outdoor lighting can attract insects, which, in turn, will draw spiders and black widows to your property. Consider using low-intensity, yellow, or sodium vapor lights, which are less appealing to insects. Additionally, try to keep exterior lights off when not needed, and use indoor curtains or blinds to prevent light from spilling outside.

  1. Prune and Maintain Landscaping

Overgrown vegetation and plants close to your home can provide hiding spots for spiders and their prey. Trim bushes, shrubs, and trees away from the house to minimize shelter and reduce the likelihood of spiders finding their way indoors. Regularly clean up fallen leaves and debris in the yard, as they can become perfect hiding spots.

  1. Use Natural Repellents

Several natural remedies can help deter spiders and black widows from invading your living space. Some popular options include:

a. Vinegar: Mix equal parts of water and white vinegar in a spray bottle and apply it to areas where spiders are likely to lurk. The acidic smell repels them effectively.

b. Essential oils: Spiders dislike strong-smelling essential oils such as peppermint, tea tree, and lavender. Dilute a few drops of essential oil in water and spray it around windows, doorways, and other entry points.

c. Citrus peels: Rub citrus peels (orange, lemon, or lime) along baseboards, windowsills, and other areas to deter spiders due to their aversion to citrus scents.

  1. Deploy Sticky Traps

For a non-toxic method of capturing and removing spiders, use sticky traps strategically placed in areas where you often spot them. Check the traps regularly, and if you capture harmless spiders, release them back into the wild away from your home.

  1. Adopt a Cat

Cats are natural hunters and may help keep your home free of pests, including spiders. Their presence alone can deter spiders from setting up webs, and they may even catch a few crawling critters themselves.

By following these tips, you can maintain a spider and black widow-free living space during the summer without resorting to harmful chemicals or lethal methods. Remember, it's essential to respect the role of spiders in the ecosystem while still ensuring the safety and comfort of your home. If you're still struggling to keep your home spider free, give Hunter's Services a call at 530-342-8950.

title: Protecting Your Home from Pests During Thanksgiving: Tips for a Pest-Free Holiday
author: Michael Valdez
desc: Protecting Your Home from Pests During Thanksgiving: Tips for a Pest-Free HolidayAs Thanksgiving approaches, the warmth of family gatherings, delicio
body:
Protecting Your Home from Pests During Thanksgiving: Tips for a Pest-Free Holiday

As Thanksgiving approaches, the warmth of family gatherings, delicious aromas wafting from the kitchen, and the joy of festivities fill the air. However, this time of year also brings about some unwanted guests—pests seeking shelter and food indoors as temperatures drop. Keeping your home pest-free during Thanksgiving requires a proactive approach. Here are some tips to ensure your celebrations remain uninterrupted by uninvited critters:

1. Seal Entry Points:

Inspect your home for any gaps or cracks in walls, windows, doors, and foundations—common entry points for pests. Use caulk or weather stripping to seal these openings and prevent pests from sneaking in. Pay special attention to areas around pipes, cables, and vents.

2. Store Food Properly:

With the abundance of delicious Thanksgiving dishes, food can be a magnet for pests. Store food in airtight containers to discourage pests like ants, rodents, and pantry moths. Keep fruits in the refrigerator and ensure that all trash cans have tight-fitting lids.

3. Clean Regularly:

Maintain a clean and clutter-free home. Regularly vacuum and sweep floors, wipe down countertops, and clean up crumbs and spills promptly. Don't forget to clean behind and under large appliances where food particles might accumulate.

4. Dispose of Trash Properly:

Dispose of garbage regularly and ensure trash bins are located away from the house. Clean the bins frequently to prevent odors that can attract pests. Consider using sealed trash cans both indoors and outdoors.

5. Outdoor Maintenance:

Trim bushes, trees, and shrubs around your home to prevent pests from using them as bridges to access your house. Clean out gutters to avoid moisture accumulation and potential nesting sites for pests.

6. Monitor for Signs of Pests:

Keep an eye out for signs of pest activity, such as droppings, gnaw marks, or strange noises in the walls. Addressing these signs early can prevent a full-blown infestation.

7. Professional Pest Control:

Consider hiring a professional pest control service for preventive treatments. Experts can identify potential problem areas and provide targeted solutions to safeguard your home against pests.

8. Inspect Decorations:

If you're using stored decorations for Thanksgiving, inspect them for any signs of pests or pest damage before bringing them inside. Pests might find their way into stored items during the year.

9. Address Moisture Issues:

Fix any leaks or moisture problems in your home. Pests like termites and cockroaches are attracted to damp areas, so keeping your home dry can discourage their presence.

Conclusion:

Protecting your home from pests during Thanksgiving involves a combination of proactive measures, regular maintenance, and vigilance. By taking these steps, you can create an environment that's less attractive to pests, ensuring a peaceful and enjoyable holiday celebration with your loved ones.

For professional assistance and comprehensive pest control solutions, consider reaching out to Hunter's Services Pest Control. Their expert team specializes in identifying potential pest problems, offering tailored treatments, and safeguarding your home against unwanted invaders.

To schedule a consultation or learn more about how Hunter's Services Pest Control can help you keep your home pest-free, call them at 530-342-8950 or visit their website at HuntersPestControl.com/contact.

As you prepare for Thanksgiving, incorporating these pest prevention strategies combined with expert guidance from Hunter's Services Pest Control can significantly reduce the risk of unwanted pests, ensuring a worry-free and delightful holiday season.

title: Top 5 Pests during the Winter Season
author: Michael Valdez
desc: Winter has its own unique charm with snowflakes falling and the promise of cozy evenings by the fireplace. However, along with the joys of the season, wint
excerpt: Winter has its own unique charm with snowflakes falling and the promise of cozy evenings by the fireplace. However, along with the joys of the season, winter al
body:
Winter has its own unique charm with snowflakes falling and the promise of cozy evenings by the fireplace. However, along with the joys of the season, winter also brings some unwelcome guests into our homes—winter pests. These pests seek shelter and sustenance indoors when the temperatures drop, making them a common nuisance during the colder months. In this blog post, we'll highlight the top five winter pests to watch out for and provide tips on how to identify and deal with them.

1. Rodents - Mice and rats are among the most notorious winter pests. They enter homes through small cracks and gaps in search of warmth and food. Signs of a rodent infestation include droppings, chewed wires, and gnawed food packaging. To prevent these pests, seal entry points, keep food stored in airtight containers, and consider setting traps or hiring a pest control professional.

2. Spiders - While many spider species remain active during the winter, some seek shelter indoors. Common household spiders like the house spider and wolf spider may be found in corners, basements, and attics. Regular cleaning and sealing cracks can help deter spiders from entering your home.

3. Cockroaches - Cockroaches are resilient pests that can survive in colder temperatures. They often hide in warm, damp areas like kitchens and bathrooms. Keep these areas clean, fix any water leaks, and store food securely to reduce the risk of a roach infestation.

4. Stink Bugs - Brown marmorated stink bugs are known for their foul odor when disturbed. They enter homes through cracks and openings in walls. To prevent stink bugs, seal gaps in your home's exterior, especially around windows and doors. Vacuuming them up is an effective removal method.

5. Silverfish - These small, silvery insects are attracted to moisture and can often be found in bathrooms and kitchens. Reduce humidity levels in your home, fix any leaks, and use a dehumidifier if necessary to discourage silverfish.

Dealing with winter pests can be challenging, but with vigilance and proactive measures, you can keep your home pest-free throughout the season. If you suspect a pest infestation that's beyond your control, don't hesitate to reach out to a professional pest control service. They have the knowledge and tools to identify, treat, and prevent winter pest problems.

Remember, the key to effective pest control is prevention. By sealing entry points, maintaining cleanliness, and addressing moisture issues, you can significantly reduce the likelihood of encountering these winter pests. Enjoy a cozy and pest-free winter season!

title: Winter Pest Control: Preparing Your Home for a Cozy and Pest-Free Season
author: Michael Valdez
desc: As the air turns crisp and the leaves change color, the arrival of winter brings with it a certain charm. However, along with the cozy fireside evenings an
excerpt: As the air turns crisp and the leaves change color, the arrival of winter brings with it a certain charm. However, along with the cozy fireside evenings and hot
body:
As the air turns crisp and the leaves change color, the arrival of winter brings with it a certain charm. However, along with the cozy fireside evenings and hot cocoa, winter can also usher in unwelcome guests in the form of pests seeking refuge from the cold. To ensure that your home remains a haven of comfort without becoming a haven for pests, it's essential to take proactive steps to prepare your home for winter pest control. In this article, we'll explore effective strategies to keep common winter pests at bay and maintain a pest-free living environment.

1. Seal Entry Points: Pests such as rodents, insects, and spiders are adept at finding their way indoors through even the tiniest openings. Take a thorough walk around your home's exterior, searching for gaps in walls, cracks in foundations, and broken seals around doors and windows. Seal these entry points using weather-stripping, caulk, or mesh screens to prevent pests from sneaking in.

2. Maintain Landscaping: Trim back any overgrown vegetation, branches, or bushes that are in close proximity to your home. Pests often use these as pathways to access your house. Rake up fallen leaves and debris, as they can create cozy hiding spots for pests like rodents.

3. Store Firewood Properly: If you use firewood, make sure to store it at least 20 feet away from your home. Pests, particularly termites and ants, can easily infest firewood and then migrate into your living space. Elevate the wood off the ground and cover it with a tarp to deter pests.

4. Clean and Declutter: Pests are attracted to food sources and clutter. Before winter sets in, thoroughly clean your kitchen, pantry, and dining areas. Store food in airtight containers and regularly dispose of garbage in sealed bins. Additionally, decluttering your home reduces hiding spots for pests, making it harder for them to settle in.

5. Inspect Attic and Basement: These areas are prime hiding spots for pests seeking warmth. Inspect your attic and basement for signs of infestations such as droppings, nests, or chewed wires. If you spot any issues, contact a pest control professional to address the problem before it escalates.

6. Repair Screens and Vents: Ensure that all screens on doors and windows are intact and free from tears or holes. Similarly, inspect vents for any gaps that pests could use as entry points. Installing mesh screens over vents can prevent pests from sneaking indoors.

7. Regularly Clean Gutters: Clogged gutters can lead to water accumulation, which not only damages your home but also provides an ideal breeding ground for pests like mosquitoes. Regularly clean your gutters to prevent water buildup and subsequent pest problems.

If you need help getting your gutters cleaned we can help! Give us a call today at 530-342-8950 or get ahold of us here.

8. Professional Pest Inspection: Consider scheduling a professional pest inspection before winter arrives. Experienced pest control experts can identify potential problem areas and offer recommendations tailored to your home's specific needs. Hunter's Services can help you get your home ready for the winter. Schedule a winter pest inspection today!

9. Natural Remedies: Consider using natural deterrents like peppermint oil, cedarwood, or diatomaceous earth to repel pests. These options are generally safer for pets, children, and the environment compared to chemical pesticides.

10. Regular Maintenance: Pest control is an ongoing process. Even after taking these steps, it's important to maintain vigilance throughout the winter months. Regularly check for signs of pests and promptly address any issues that arise.

As winter settles in, ensuring your home is properly prepared for pest control is essential for a cozy and stress-free season. By sealing entry points, maintaining your landscaping, and implementing proactive measures, you can enjoy a pest-free living environment throughout the winter months. Remember, a little preparation goes a long way in keeping your home pest-free and your winter spirits high.

If you still find yourself needing help getting your home ready for the winter or want to have one of our pest control professionals come out and take a look, give us a call at 530-342-8950 or contact us here.

title: Winter Weed Treatments: Your Secret Weapon Against Spring Overgrowth
author: Michael Valdez
desc: As the winter season settles in and landscapes are blanketed by snow, it might seem counterintuitive to consider weed control. However, strategizing and im
excerpt: As the winter season settles in and landscapes are blanketed by snow, it might seem counterintuitive to consider weed control. However, strategizing and impleme
body:
As the winter season settles in and landscapes are blanketed by snow, it might seem counterintuitive to consider weed control. However, strategizing and implementing weed treatments during winter can be your most potent defense against the impending battle with springtime weeds.

The Winter Weed Conundrum

Winter might give the illusion of a dormant period for plants, but beneath the surface, weed seeds lie in wait. They're resilient and opportunistic, ready to sprout and proliferate as soon as the weather warms. This silent growth can transform into a full-blown weed invasion, making your yard a battleground in the coming spring.

The Advantages of Winter Treatments

1. Weed Prevention: Applying treatments in winter targets weed seeds before they germinate. This proactive approach halts their growth, reducing the number of weeds in your landscape.

2. Environmental Impact: Winter treatments often require less chemical use due to the reduced weed population, which can contribute to a more eco-friendly approach to weed control.

3. Healthier Landscapes: By nipping weeds in the bud during winter, you give your desired plants a competitive advantage. They can thrive without the relentless competition from invasive weeds.

4. Time and Cost Efficiency: Preventive measures in winter often save time and money in the long run. Controlling weeds before they proliferate means less effort and resources required for extensive weed removal later.

Effective Winter Treatments

1. Pre-Emergent Herbicides: These specialized products target weed seeds, inhibiting their growth without harming established plants. Application timing is critical, and winter is the ideal time to deploy these solutions.

2. Mulching and Covering: Using mulch or ground covers in winter acts as a physical barrier, preventing weed seeds from reaching the soil and germinating.

3. Professional Assistance: Engaging with a professional pest control service can ensure a tailored approach to your specific landscape needs. They can employ the right treatments at the right time for maximum efficacy.

Take Action Now

Don’t wait for the arrival of spring to address potential weed problems. Act in the winter to thwart their growth and maintain a healthy, vibrant landscape all year round.

Contact Hunter's Services for Expert Assistance

To safeguard your property from invasive weeds and ensure a beautiful landscape come springtime, reach out to Hunter's Services. Visit our website at www.hunterspestcontrol.com or call us at 530-342-8950. Our team of experts is dedicated to providing tailored solutions to keep your outdoor spaces weed-free and flourishing.

By taking proactive steps during winter, you're setting the stage for a weed-free and thriving landscape when the warmer seasons arrive.

title: Keeping Your Yard Pest-Free During the Summer: Effective Strategies for a Peaceful Outdoor Haven
author: Michael Valdez
desc: As the warm embrace of summer arrives, it brings with it longer days, blooming flowers, and vibrant greenery. However, this delightful season also attracts
excerpt: As the warm embrace of summer arrives, it brings with it longer days, blooming flowers, and vibrant greenery. However, this delightful season also attracts an a
body:
As the warm embrace of summer arrives, it brings with it longer days, blooming flowers, and vibrant greenery. However, this delightful season also attracts an array of unwanted pests that can disrupt the tranquility of your yard. From buzzing mosquitoes to pesky ants and curious critters, these intruders can turn your outdoor oasis into a constant battle. Fear not! With a few simple strategies and preventive measures, you can keep pests at bay and enjoy a pest-free summer in your yard. In this blog post, we will explore effective ways to safeguard your outdoor space and create an inviting haven for relaxation and enjoyment.

  1. Eliminate Standing Water: Mosquitoes are notorious summer pests that thrive in standing water. To minimize their presence, inspect your yard for any sources of stagnant water, such as birdbaths, clogged gutters, or empty containers. Regularly empty and clean these areas to prevent mosquitoes from laying eggs and breeding. Additionally, consider using mosquito dunks or installing a fountain or pond aerator to disrupt their life cycle.

  2. Maintain a Tidy Yard: Pests find refuge in unkempt yards. Regular maintenance and cleanliness are key to reducing their presence. Keep your grass neatly trimmed, remove weeds and overgrown vegetation, and promptly dispose of fallen leaves and debris. By eliminating potential hiding spots and nesting areas, you discourage pests from making your yard their home.

  3. Seal Entry Points: Critters like rodents and insects often enter your yard through small gaps and cracks. Inspect your property for potential entry points, such as gaps around doors and windows, openings in your foundation, or gaps in fences. Seal these gaps using caulk, weatherstripping, or appropriate sealants to prevent pests from infiltrating your yard.

  4. Utilize Natural Pest Deterrents: Nature offers a plethora of pest-repelling options that are both effective and eco-friendly. Consider planting natural deterrents like marigolds, lavender, mint, and citronella around your yard. These plants emit scents that repel mosquitoes, ants, and other insects. Additionally, certain herbs like basil, rosemary, and lemongrass can deter flies and gnats. Incorporate these plants strategically in your garden beds, near seating areas, or in pots to create a pest-discouraging barrier.

  5. Use Protective Netting and Screens: If your yard is prone to mosquito or fly infestations, consider installing protective netting or screens around seating areas, patios, and gazebos. This physical barrier prevents pests from reaching you while allowing for an uninterrupted outdoor experience. Make sure the screens are in good condition and repair any tears or holes to ensure maximum effectiveness.

  6. Regularly Empty and Clean Garbage Bins: Proper waste management is essential in deterring pests. Ensure your garbage bins have tightly sealed lids and are regularly emptied. Clean the bins periodically to eliminate any food residue that might attract insects, rodents, or raccoons. If possible, keep the bins away from frequently used areas to minimize the odor and presence of pests.

  7. Install Outdoor Lighting Strategically: While outdoor lighting enhances the ambiance of your yard, it can also attract insects. Opt for yellow or sodium vapor lights instead of traditional white lights, as they are less appealing to bugs. Additionally, position lights away from seating areas and entrances to minimize insect activity near high-traffic zones.

  8. Call Hunters Services: While all these methods are great for keeping your backyard in tip top shape for the summer months, sometimes you just need a little extra help. We have summer specials going on right now for as low as $40 a month. Our trained technicians use Eco Certified products to ensure the safety of your family and plants.

If you're needing help getting your garden in shape and need an experts help, give us a call today at 530-342-8950 or get ahold of us here.

title: The Ultimate Guide to Keeping Ants Out of Your Home
author: Michael Valdez
desc: Dealing with ants invading your home can be a frustrating and common problem for many homeowners. While ants play an important role in the ecosystem, they
excerpt: Dealing with ants invading your home can be a frustrating and common problem for many homeowners. While ants play an important role in the ecosystem, they can q
body:
Dealing with ants invading your home can be a frustrating and common problem for many homeowners. While ants play an important role in the ecosystem, they can quickly become a nuisance when they start to march across your kitchen countertops or invade your pantry. Fortunately, there are several effective strategies you can employ to keep ants out of your home without resorting to harmful chemicals or expensive exterminators. In this blog post, we will explore some simple yet effective methods to keep ants at bay and maintain a clean and ant-free living space.

  1. Seal Entry Points: Ants can find their way into your home through even the tiniest cracks and gaps. Conduct a thorough inspection of your home's exterior and seal any potential entry points. Use caulk to seal gaps around windows and doors, and install door sweeps to prevent ants from squeezing through the gaps at the bottom of doors. Pay special attention to areas where utility pipes or cables enter your home, as ants can use these as pathways as well.

  2. Keep a Clean and Tidy Home: One of the most crucial steps in preventing ant infestations is maintaining a clean living environment. Wipe down surfaces regularly to eliminate food crumbs and spills that may attract ants. Sweep and vacuum your floors regularly, paying attention to hidden corners and under appliances. Make sure to store food in airtight containers, and promptly clean up any food or drink spills to avoid tempting ant scouts.

  3. Eliminate Food Sources: Ants are primarily attracted to food sources, so it's important to remove their access to potential meals. Keep your kitchen tidy by wiping down countertops, stovetops, and tables after each use. Avoid leaving unwashed dishes in the sink overnight, as food residue can attract ants. Additionally, make sure to dispose of garbage promptly and keep trash cans tightly sealed to prevent odors from attracting ants.

  4. Natural Ant Deterrents: Several natural substances act as effective ant deterrents. Consider using them strategically to keep ants away from your home. For instance, sprinkle cinnamon, cayenne pepper, or coffee grounds around entry points, windowsills, and other areas where ants are likely to crawl. These substances interfere with the ants' scent trails, making it harder for them to navigate and locate food sources.

  5. Create Barriers: Creating physical barriers can prevent ants from entering your home. Ants are reluctant to cross certain substances, such as chalk, baby powder, or diatomaceous earth. Draw a line with chalk or sprinkle a thin layer of baby powder or diatomaceous earth along windowsills, doorways, and any other points of entry. These barriers disrupt the ants' movement and discourage them from venturing further into your home.

  6. Trim Trees and Plants: Sometimes ants find their way into your home by using tree branches or plants as bridges. Trim back branches that touch your house, and ensure there is a reasonable distance between plants and the exterior walls. By eliminating direct access routes, you can significantly reduce the chances of ants finding their way indoors.

  7. Seek Professional Help if Needed: If your ant problem persists despite your best efforts, it may be time to seek professional assistance. Hunters Services have the knowledge and experience to identify the ant species and develop targeted strategies to eliminate them from your home. We can provide you with tailored advice and safe treatments to effectively address the issue.

Keeping ants out of your home requires a proactive approach and a combination of preventive measures. By sealing entry points, maintaining cleanliness, eliminating food sources, and utilizing natural deterrents, you can successfully discourage ants from invading your living space. Remember that consistency is key, and regular maintenance will help ensure an ant-free environment. With these simple yet effective tips, you can reclaim your home and keep ants at bay all year round.

title: Why Hiring a Year-Round Pest Control Service Will Keep Your Home Safe
author: Michael Valdez
desc: When it comes to safeguarding your home, ensuring a pest-free environment is a top priority. Pests can wreak havoc on your property, causing structural dam
excerpt: When it comes to safeguarding your home, ensuring a pest-free environment is a top priority. Pests can wreak havoc on your property, causing structural damage,
body:
When it comes to safeguarding your home, ensuring a pest-free environment is a top priority. Pests can wreak havoc on your property, causing structural damage, health concerns, and a general sense of discomfort. While many homeowners opt for one-time pest control treatments when they notice a problem, there's a strong case to be made for investing in a year-round pest control service. In this blog post, we'll explore the various reasons why hiring a year-round pest control service is essential for keeping your home safe.

Early Detection and Prevention

One of the primary benefits of a year-round pest control service is early detection and prevention. By engaging professionals to regularly inspect and treat your property, you can identify pest issues before they become full-blown infestations. Early detection allows for a quicker response, saving you time, money, and stress.

Customized Treatment Plans

Year-round pest control services provide customized treatment plans tailored to your specific needs. Professionals will consider your geographical location, the type of pests common to your area, and the unique characteristics of your home. This personalized approach ensures that your property receives the most effective and efficient treatment.

Consistent Monitoring

Pest control is not a one-and-done task. Pests can reappear, and new threats can emerge throughout the year. A year-round pest control service ensures consistent monitoring and treatment, creating a protective barrier around your home. This ongoing attention reduces the risk of pest-related surprises.

Protecting Your Investment

Your home is one of the most significant investments you'll ever make. Pests such as termites, rodents, and carpenter ants can cause substantial damage to the structure of your property. Year-round pest control services help protect your investment by preventing costly repairs and preserving the value of your home.

Health and Safety

Pests pose a significant health and safety risk to you and your family. Rodents and insects can carry diseases, contaminate food, and trigger allergies. By maintaining a pest-free environment throughout the year, you reduce the risk of health-related issues and create a safer home for everyone.

Peace of Mind

Living with the constant worry of a pest infestation can be stressful. Year-round pest control services provide peace of mind, allowing you to enjoy your home without the looming threat of pests. You'll be able to relax and focus on other aspects of your life, knowing that professionals are taking care of your pest control needs.

Sustainable and Environmentally Friendly Solutions

Many year-round pest control services offer sustainable and environmentally friendly solutions. They use targeted treatments that minimize the impact on non-target organisms and the surrounding ecosystem. This eco-conscious approach not only protects your home but also contributes to the well-being of the environment.

Cost-Effective in the Long Run

While it may seem like a year-round pest control service is an additional expense, it can save you money in the long run. Preventing infestations and addressing issues early reduces the need for extensive, expensive treatments. By investing in proactive pest control, you can avoid costly repairs and health-related expenses.

Conclusion

Hiring a year-round pest control service is a proactive and practical approach to ensuring the safety and well-being of your home. The benefits of early detection, customized treatment plans, consistent monitoring, and the peace of mind it provides far outweigh the initial investment. With a year-round pest control service, you can protect your home, your health, and your finances while enjoying a pest-free living environment. So, take the necessary steps to safeguard your home and keep it safe from unwanted intruders year-round. Call Hunters Services today at 530-342-8950 and get $50 off your initial start up fee on year-round pest control.
